document.purpose: Synthetic policy pack for testing framework compliance scoring and evidence extraction.

scoring.scope: Scores are calculated only across the sampled controls listed in this ground truth file.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: Entity X has an approved cybersecurity strategy for 2026-2028.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: The policy states that a cybersecurity strategy exists and is approved.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: All cybersecurity policies are reviewed at least annually or after material changes in regulation, technology, business operations, or threat landscape.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: The document defines a periodic review cycle.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: A Cybersecurity Steering Committee meets quarterly to review risk posture, project progress, incidents, and compliance obligations.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: A cybersecurity governance committee is defined.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: The CISO owns this policy pack and reports cybersecurity status to the Executive Committee every quarter.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Policy ownership and communication path are documented.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: The current governance policy does not define a formal enterprise cyber risk appetite statement approved by the Board.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Risk governance exists, but formal board-approved appetite is missing.
NCA ECC 2024.gap: Define and approve enterprise cyber risk appetite.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: Entity X maintains an inventory of laptops, servers, network devices, cloud workloads, business applications, and SaaS services.
All users must have unique user IDs.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Identity and access control requirements are documented.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: Entity X has not fully implemented automated joiner-mover-leaver workflows.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Access lifecycle exists but is not fully automated or consistently controlled.
NCA ECC 2024.gap: Complete joiner-mover-leaver automation and enforce deprovisioning SLAs.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: The cryptography standard does not identify Saudi national cryptographic requirements or a locally approved algorithm catalogue.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Encryption requirements exist, but local/national cryptographic requirements are missing.
NCA ECC 2024.gap: Add locally approved cryptographic requirements and algorithm catalogue.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: Critical systems are backed up daily.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Backup frequency is defined for critical systems.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: Entity X has not performed an independent penetration test during the last twelve months.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Vulnerability scans exist, but independent penetration testing is missing.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: Critical security logs are retained online for 90 days and archived for 180 days.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Logging exists, but retention is shorter than stronger expectations for critical logs.

NCA ECC 2024.evidence: Entity X maintains an incident response plan with severity levels, escalation contacts, communication channels, and decision authority.
Entity X does not yet perform periodic security reassessments for all active vendors after onboarding.
NCA ECC 2024.rationale: Third-party onboarding exists, but ongoing monitoring is incomplete.
The policy pack does not include a complete information/data classification program.
NCA ECC 2024.gap: Complete data classification across all information assets.

NCA ECC 2024.rationale: The document does not define secure email service requirements.

NIST CSF 2.0.evidence: The program applies to all employees, contractors, systems, cloud services, and third-party connections that process Entity X information.

NIST CSF 2.0.gap: Define risk appetite and tolerance statements.

NIST CSF 2.0.evidence: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities are documented for the Board Sponsor, CISO, IT Operations Manager, System Owners, Data Owners, and Employees.
Entity X maintains an Information Security Management Program approved by the Executive Committee.
Entity X maintains an inventory of laptops, servers, network devices, cloud workloads, business applications, and SaaS services.
Each asset has an owner, location, criticality rating, and lifecycle status.
NIST CSF 2.0.gap: Complete classification for all information assets.

NIST CSF 2.0.evidence: All users must have unique user IDs.
Administrative access, VPN access, cloud console access, and remote access require multi-factor authentication.
Sensitive data at rest must use AES-256 or an equivalent approved encryption mechanism.
Critical vulnerabilities on internet-facing systems must be remediated within 15 calendar days.
NIST CSF 2.0.gap: Add complete patch performance measurement and exception trend reporting.

NIST CSF 2.0.evidence: Security logs from firewalls, servers, identity providers, endpoint protection, and critical applications are forwarded to a centralized logging platform.
NIST CSF 2.0.gap: Extend log retention and monitoring coverage.

NIST CSF 2.0.evidence: Security incidents must be reported to the CISO within 24 hours of discovery.
Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) values are documented for critical systems.
NIST CSF 2.0.gap: Add a complete recovery plan covering communications, owners, and post-incident restoration steps.

NIST CSF 2.0.evidence: Vendors handling sensitive data complete a security questionnaire before onboarding.

SAMA CSF 1.0.evidence: A Cybersecurity Steering Committee meets quarterly to review risk posture, project progress, incidents, and compliance obligations.
Entity X has an approved cybersecurity strategy for 2026-2028.
Entity X maintains an Information Security Management Program approved by the Executive Committee.
All employees complete cybersecurity awareness training during onboarding and annually thereafter.
The current governance policy does not define a formal enterprise cyber risk appetite statement approved by the Board.
SAMA CSF 1.0.gap: Define formal risk appetite and KRIs.
Add independent cyber audit scope, frequency, and reporting process.

SAMA CSF 1.0.evidence: Entity X has not completed a formal data classification register for all information assets.
User access reviews are performed quarterly for critical systems and semi-annually for non-critical systems.
The cryptography standard does not identify Saudi national cryptographic requirements or a locally approved algorithm catalogue.
The policy does not define a formal digital forensics evidence-handling procedure or external forensic retainer.
Third-party contracts for technology services include confidentiality, security incident notification, access control, and right-to-audit clauses.

SAMA CSF 1.0.gap: Add detailed outsourcing governance and ongoing oversight requirements.

ISO 27001 reference.evidence: Entity X maintains an Information Security Management Program approved by the Executive Committee.
The CISO owns this policy pack and reports cybersecurity status to the Executive Committee every quarter.
Entity X maintains an inventory of laptops, servers, network devices, cloud workloads, business applications, and SaaS services.
Classification is currently limited to customer records and finance data.
New access requests require manager approval and system-owner approval before provisioning.
Passwords must be at least 12 characters, block common passwords, and be changed immediately after suspected compromise.
Cloud services must be approved by the CISO and IT Operations before production use.
ISO 27001 reference.gap: Add cloud configuration baseline and monitoring requirements.

ISO 27001 reference.evidence: Entity X maintains an incident response plan with severity levels, escalation contacts, communication channels, and decision authority.
Critical systems are backed up daily.
Critical security logs are retained online for 90 days and archived for 180 days.

ISO 27001 reference.gap: Add ongoing third-party supplier monitoring requirements.
